[2024-04-25 20:31] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): gj! my main call outs would be:

1. you need a different build order if you are going for fab, you floated too much gold in the opener. I think as P1 if you want to rush fab you do 1.DD 2.CC 3.DD 4.Fab

But in this set I wouldn't try going breachproof. Mahar is not a breachproof unit anyway

For me the main units are Xeno Plasma Fab

I would buy Fab as an efficient first attacker/econ unit (it is a way to avoid ramming into absorb as early as you otherwise would) and then Xeno as best absorber and Plasma as best post-absorb threshold attack


2. You were buying Drones late into the game and then holding them next turn, also you bought an Animus in the lategame and made a Rhino with it the turn after. Not efficient. I'd say plan your defense for next turn if you can. Take your time, play slower time controls and practice counting in advance
[2024-04-25 20:32] aa01blue: I think Mahar actually favors the standard player because it absorbs for 5. Still not sure if plasmafier makes breachproof better or worse

[2024-04-25 20:36] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): In which case you use Tarsiers as the delayed attack to not ram into absorb, which is ofc never terrible
[2024-04-25 20:36] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): and then maybe go Feral -> Xeno -> Plasma? -> Hounds
[2024-04-25 20:37] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): if they go for Fab you should have room to do all that

[2024-04-29 13:18] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): here's an inelegant solution to the p1/p2 problem:

every game you play 2 games, one as p1 and one as p2

you don't see your opponent's turn until you both submit your turns in the two games

whenever you end your turn, you swap over to the other game (and maybe you can swap between the games at will while you await your opponent to end their turn)
[2024-04-29 13:19] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): it's inelegant as you have much less breathing room and have to constantly play, and you have to split your mind across two games
[2024-04-29 13:19] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): but it's as close to being fair as you can probably get without new mechanics
[2024-04-29 13:20] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): well I guess implementing this is technically new mechanics, but
[2024-04-29 13:21] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): the problem with playing the games back to back as opposed to simultaneously is that the players can learn from each other for the next game
[2024-04-29 13:21] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): which will still include some level of "unfairness" compared to the simultaneous version
[2024-04-29 13:22] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): though a back to back version would likely still be more fair than the current iteration, for some sets
[2024-04-29 13:24] Deleted User (Deleted User): and if the score is 1-1 you could have the tiebreaker be the number of turns
[2024-04-29 13:25] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): or just have it be a draw
[2024-04-29 13:25] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): otherwise you incentivize rushes over econ
[2024-04-29 13:25] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): which I don't think there should be an incentive for either

[2024-04-30 10:32] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): be ready to enjoy pot in 10 hours
[2024-04-30 10:33] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): didn't mean @ new person xd
[2024-04-30 10:33] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): though ofc they are welcome too
[2024-04-30 10:35] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): as for tips and tricks, as awa said, buying the biggest absorber is important if you can

absorb generates a ton of value over time, in some ways it is the best bang for your buck

and this game is all about figuring out how to generate the most bang for your buck
[2024-04-30 10:37] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): with that in mind, to succeed you want to have a mindset of minmaxing your resources and investments
[2024-04-30 10:37] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): float as few resources as possible, while investing them into the most efficient things possible
[2024-04-30 10:40] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): in the "standard style" of approaching a game:

* in the early game that is going to be economy units (usually drones),
* in the mid-game that is going to be attackers and soak (defense units to protect your absorber and to not get breached)
* and as you draw towards the end-game neither side will be able to afford to buy any more attack, and will have to devote all their resources to defending -- around this time drones start becoming less and less useful and you will find yourself using forcefields, or even holding drones (not mining with them but rather using their 1 HP as soak) -- this is because defending your absorb and not getting breached is very important especially when you have a "squishy" backline (for example Tarsiers are 1 health attackers, so they are very squishy)

```Episode 1: Outbreak [EXPERT]

01_Junkers.................MEDIUM: Several backtracks to figure out the trick
02_Squarewave..............EASY: One backtrack to optimize the ending
03_Overheated..............EASY: Maybe a few short backtracks here or there, but very straightforward overall
04_Omega_Factor............HARD: A bunch of wasted time trying a certain line, then backtracking to a different start, then a whole lot of backtracking in that line until getting close, and backtracking again in the late-game to the win

05_The_Solar_Fields........EASY: A little bit of backtracking until finding the line that does what I want, but had the correct idea from the beginning
06_Scarp_and_Scrutiny......EASY: Trivial until phase #4, then momentary confusion but quickly resolved
07_VILE....................EASY: Trivial with ranked experience -- one backtrack to solve late-game defense issue
08_Comm-Net................MEDIUM: A little bit of wasted time trying a certain line, backtracking a few times to try different lines, until settling on a line which quickly leads to victory with minimal effort

09_Incendiary_Biorobotics..EASY: A little bit of backtracking in the opening until finding a stable line which quickly (well, with tons of clicking) leads to victory with minimal mental effort (and high physical effort...)
```

[2024-05-04 22:16] .holyfire (Holy-Fire): Catching up on chat so responding to this message as I see it

Allowing for two games to be played simultaneously would require some serious additional coding on both client and server side, so I wouldn't expect it anytime soon. But, if such a mechanism is ever implemented, it would be useful for much more than just the P1/P2 balance you suggested.

Anyway, I believe I mentioned this before, but IMO the ideal mechanism for balancing P1/P2 is cake cutting. One player is randomly assigned to be the cutter - they examine the set and decide which handicap would be required to make it even. For example, they may decide that P2 is better, but it would be even if P1 gets an extra gold on turn 4. Then the other player is the chooser - they can decide if they want to be P1 or P2 with the selected handicap.

It is in the cutter's best interest to choose a fair handicap (otherwise the other player will choose the better side), so assuming they know what they're doing, the game will be fair (and if they don't, they get penalized for it - cutting wisely would be part of the skill of the game).
